name: AUSNUT 2011-13
api_notes: CSV bulk download only — no API. FSANZ publishes as Excel/CSV files. Values already per 100g.
opus_reliability: Gold standard. Australian government dataset maintained by FSANZ. Most comprehensive AU nutrient database with ~5700 foods. Values are laboratory-analysed, not label-derived.
opus_conflicts: Dataset is from 2011-13 — some branded products may have reformulated since. Cross-reference with current labels for branded items. Generic/unbranded foods (e.g. 'apple, raw') remain reliable.

name: Open Food Facts AU
api_notes: Free REST API, no auth required. Search by barcode: /product/{barcode}.json. Search by name: /cgi/search.pl?search_terms={name}&cc=au. Rate limit: ~100 req/min.
opus_reliability: Community-maintained with strong AU coverage for branded/packaged foods. Barcode-to-nutrition mapping is the primary value. Data quality varies — some entries are OCR'd from labels with errors.
opus_conflicts: Nutrition values may differ from AUSNUT for the same product due to label vs lab analysis differences. Always prefer AUSNUT for generic foods. OFF is strongest for barcode-identified branded products not in AUSNUT.

name: GI Foundation AU
api_notes: No public API. GI values available via their searchable database at gisymbol.com. Manual lookup or scraping required. Provides GI rating (low/medium/high) and some carb data.
opus_reliability: Authoritative source for glycemic index values in Australia. GI Symbol Program tests are laboratory-certified. Carb data cross-referenced with AUSNUT.
opus_conflicts: Limited product coverage — only foods enrolled in the GI Symbol Program. Carb values should match AUSNUT for overlapping products. Primary value is the gi_rating field, not nutrition data.

name: Diabetes Australia
api_notes: No API. Nutrition guidance available on website. Manual extraction from educational materials and food guides.
opus_reliability: Disease-specific nutrition guidance from Australia's peak diabetes body. Carb-focused content designed for diabetes management. Information is curated by dietitians.
opus_conflicts: Provides ranges and guidance rather than precise per-product values. Best used to validate categories and general carb ranges, not as a primary data source for specific products.

name: Eat For Health (NHMRC)
api_notes: No API. Australian Dietary Guidelines published by NHMRC. Nutrient reference values and food group guidance available as downloadable documents.
opus_reliability: Government-published national dietary guidelines. General nutrition recommendations, not product-specific data. Useful for category validation and serve size reference.
opus_conflicts: Focuses on food groups and serve sizes rather than specific branded products. Serve sizes may differ from manufacturer-stated serves. Not suitable as a primary carb data source.

name: FoodWorks Professional
api_notes: Commercial licensed software by Xyris. No public API — requires desktop license. Uses AUSNUT as base dataset with additional branded foods and recipe analysis.
opus_reliability: Dietitian-grade database built on AUSNUT with ~4000 additional branded foods. Laboratory-quality data. Used by hospitals and dietetic practices across Australia.
opus_conflicts: Commercial license required — cannot bulk-extract data. Useful for manual verification of specific products. Base dataset overlaps with AUSNUT; branded additions may not have public equivalents.

name: CalorieKing AU
api_notes: Web search interface, no public API. AU-specific version with local branded foods. Manual lookup required.
opus_reliability: Commercial database with reasonable AU branded food coverage. Data sourced from manufacturer labels. Popular with consumers but not laboratory-verified.
opus_conflicts: Label-derived values may differ from AUSNUT lab values. Some entries may be outdated if products have been reformulated. Use as supplementary source only — cross-reference with Tier 1.

name: NUTTAB 2010
api_notes: CSV bulk download from FSANZ. Predecessor to AUSNUT 2011-13. Same format but older dataset.
opus_reliability: Government dataset but superseded by AUSNUT 2011-13. Still useful for foods not covered in AUSNUT or for historical comparison. Laboratory-analysed values.
opus_conflicts: Older dataset — use only for foods absent from AUSNUT 2011-13. Where both datasets cover the same food, AUSNUT values take precedence. Some foods may have been reformulated since 2010.

name: Nutritionix API
api_notes: REST API, requires API key (free tier available). US-centric database. Endpoints: /natural/nutrients (NLP query), /search/instant (autocomplete). Rate limit: 50 req/day (free).
opus_reliability: Large database (~1M items) but US-focused. Has some AU products via barcode matching. NLP query endpoint is convenient but may return US equivalents instead of AU products.
opus_conflicts: US nutrition labelling uses calories (kcal) not kJ — conversion required (kcal * 4.184). Serving sizes follow US standards, not AU. Lowest confidence tier — use only when AU sources have no data.
